description:
Congressman David Scott (GA-13), senior member of the House Financial Services Committee and House Agriculture Committee, seeks a Body Person/Legislative Aide to join his Washington, D.C. office.

This role is highly collaborative and Member-facing.
responsibilities: Staffing the Congressman on and off the Hill for events and votes
Coordinating closely with the Member's Director of Operations and other operations staff to ensure timely arrival of all appointments
Overseeing a policy portfolio including energy, environment, defense, healthcare, civil rights, and others as assigned
Researching and advancing legislative and policy priorities
Drafting vote recommendations, memos, and talking points
Monitoring legislation and Floor activity
Representing the Congressman in meetings with constituents, advocates, and stakeholder groups
Assisting the Staff Assistant/Legislative Correspondent with front office operations as needed
qualifications: Ideal candidate will be a team player with a strong sense of judgement, flexibility, and discretion
Ability to work in a highly collaborative and Member-facing environment
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should email a cover letter, resume, and one writing sample to Georgia13Resumes@gmail.com with the subject line, 'Last Name, First Name, Body Person/Legislative Aide'. The deadline to apply is Friday, March 27th, COB.
salary_info: Salary range is commensurate with experience and includes health insurance, student loan repayment, and other benefits.
equal_opportunity: The Office of Congressman David Scott is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, or military service. Diverse candidates are encouraged to apply.

description:
The Office of Congressman Alma S. Adams, Ph.D. seeks a Legislative Director. The LD will help drive the proactive legislative agenda for the office, developing and implementing the strategy to push policy priorities and legislative goals; supervise and mentor the legislative staff while advising on the Congresswoman's legislative portfolio, and manage their own legislative portfolio.

The LD will work closely in conjunction with the Chief of Staff and senior leadership team to ensure legislative strategy is incorporated into overall operations. The LD will also help ensure coordination between DC and district offices on important legislative updates and district-based events and projects.

This is not an entry level position; extensive Hill experience and knowledge of the legislative process are required. The successful applicant will have crisp and thorough written and oral communications skills, an entrepreneurial and self-starting attitude, strong attention to detail, creativity, and be a team player. Supervisory experience and expertise in Agricultural policy is required.
responsibilities: Drive the proactive legislative agenda for the office
Develop and implement the strategy to push policy priorities and legislative goals
Supervise and mentor the legislative staff
Advise on the Congresswoman's legislative portfolio
Manage their own legislative portfolio
Work closely with the Chief of Staff and senior leadership team to ensure legislative strategy is incorporated into overall operations
Ensure coordination between DC and district offices on important legislative updates and district-based events and projects
qualifications: Extensive Hill experience required
Knowledge of the legislative process required
Crisp and thorough written and oral communications skills
Entrepreneurial and self-starting attitude
Strong attention to detail
Creativity
Team player
Supervisory experience required
Expertise in Agricultural policy required
how_to_apply: Qualified applicants should submit a cover letter, resume, and two writing samples to nc12.resume@mail.house.gov. Candidates from BIPOC, LGBT+, working class, and/or other underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply, as well as candidates with North Carolina ties. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we will only contact you if we decide to move forward with your application. Please no calls, direct emails, or walk-ins.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity, or sexual orientation.

description: Congresswoman Emilia Sykes (OH-13) is seeking an enthusiastic and creative Temporary Communications Assistant to work in her Akron, Ohio office full-time from mid-June to the end of October, 2026. This position will work closely with the communications director and the district-based team. Excellent writing, verbal and organizational skills are essential along with the ability to be a self-starter while working in a fast-paced environment. Job responsibilities include editing videos, creating graphics, photographing events, writing press releases and short speeches, and other duties as assigned. Previous communications experience is required.
responsibilities: Edit videos
Create graphics
Photograph events
Write press releases and short speeches
Perform other duties as assigned
qualifications: Excellent writing, verbal, and organizational skills
Ability to be a self-starter
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
Previous communications experience required
how_to_apply: Qualified candidates should submit a resume, one writing sample, and one creative sample such as a video or graphic you've created, to JoinTeamSykes@mail.house.gov with "Temporary Communications Assistant" in the Subject Line. Applications will be accepted until the end of business on Friday, April 17. No phone calls, faxes, or drop-ins please. This position is based in Akron, Ohio.

description:
The Office of Congresswoman Maggie Goodlander (NH-02) seeks a dynamic Outreach Coordinator to join the team. This role is central to the Congresswoman's engagement with communities across New Hampshire and requires strong judgment, excellent follow-through, and a deep commitment to constituent service. The Outreach Coordinator will represent the Congresswoman in meetings and community settings, manage an assigned outreach portfolio, and help ensure seamless coordination between the district and Washington, D.C. teams.

We are looking for a highly organized, people-oriented professional who is comfortable engaging with diverse stakeholders, staffing the Congresswoman at events, and translating community conversations into meaningful follow-up and action. The ideal candidate is a strong communicator, thrives in a fast-paced environment, and brings attention to detail, initiative, and professionalism to every interaction.

This position is based in Concord, New Hampshire, with frequent travel across the district and regular evening and weekend work required. A valid driver's license is required. Prior Hill, government, or community engagement experience is preferred but not required. New Hampshire ties are a plus. Salary range is $55,000-$70,000, commensurate with experience.
responsibilities: Represent the Congresswoman in meetings and community settings
Manage an assigned outreach portfolio
Ensure seamless coordination between the district and Washington, D.C. teams
Engage with diverse stakeholders
Staff the Congresswoman at events
Translate community conversations into meaningful follow-up and action
qualifications: Strong judgment and excellent follow-through
Deep commitment to constituent service
Highly organized, people-oriented professional
Strong communicator
Thrives in a fast-paced environment
Attention to detail, initiative, and professionalism
Valid driver's license required
Prior Hill, government, or community engagement experience preferred
New Hampshire ties are a plus
how_to_apply: Submit their applications at the following link by April 3, 2026: https://forms.gle/VndWP6oNvnjnHKzW6

description: The Office of Congressman Brad Sherman (CA-32) is seeking to fill a job opening for a Caseworker / Staff Assistant position in the Sherman Oaks district office. The ideal applicant is organized, professional, highly motivated, and able to work in a fast-paced environment. This is an entry-level position.
responsibilities: Interact with constituents via phone, email and in-office meetings
Assist senior staff with administrative responsibilities
Input, file and review constituent inquiries
Contact federal agencies on behalf of constituents
Represent the office at district events
qualifications: Organized, professional, and highly motivated
Strong writing, typing and interpersonal skills
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and meet deadlines
Proficiency with Microsoft Office and database software preferred
Bachelor's degree from an accredited four-year college or university
Past experience in a social work or government field preferred
Ability to work some evenings and weekends
how_to_apply: Please submit cover letter and resume attn: Scott Abrams to scott.abrams@mail.house.gov No calls please.
salary_info: $45,000 per year. We offer a generous student loan reimbursement package.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation.

position_title: Professional Staff Member / Senior Professional Staff Member
office: U.S. House Committee on Homeland Security (Majority), Subcommittee on Oversight, Investigations, and Accountability
description:
The U.S. House Committee on Homeland Security (Majority) is seeking a Professional Staff Member/ Senior Professional Staff Member to serve on the Subcommittee on Oversight, Investigations, and Accountability.

The PSM serves as a key policy advisor and legislative strategist within the Subcommittee, reporting directly to the Subcommittee Director. This role supports the Subcommittee's work by conducting in-depth policy research and investigations, developing legislative proposals, and contributing to the planning and execution of hearings, briefings, and other oversight activities.

The PSM is expected to develop and maintain subject-matter expertise in areas within the Subcommittee's jurisdiction, particularly those involving agencies and offices within the Department of Homeland Security. Responsibilities include tracking agency activities, identifying policy gaps, and reviewing and analyzing reports, regulations, and other developments relevant to the Subcommittee's priorities.

Working collaboratively with the Subcommittee Director, the Committee's Policy Director, and legal counsel, the PSM assists in drafting legislation, preparing hearing materials and Member questions, and producing background memoranda and briefing documents for Members and senior staff. Strong attention to detail, excellent written and verbal communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple competing priorities under tight deadlines are essential for success in this role.

Should an applicant possess more senior experience, the position of Senior Professional Staff Member will be considered. A Senior PSM serves as a strategic advisor and trusted counselor to the Subcommittee Director on a wide range of policy and procedural matters within the Subcommittee's jurisdiction and may be called upon to manage Subcommittee operations in the Director's absence. Senior PSMs are expected to maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders, including congressional leadership and industry representatives, possess a deep understanding of the legislative process, and demonstrate the ability to analyze and engage with the Department's annual budget.
responsibilities: Conduct in-depth policy research and investigations
Develop legislative proposals
Contribute to the planning and execution of hearings, briefings, and other oversight activities
Track agency activities and identify policy gaps
Review and analyze reports, regulations, and other developments relevant to the Subcommittee's priorities
Draft legislation, prepare hearing materials and Member questions, and produce background memoranda and briefing documents
Serve as a strategic advisor and trusted counselor to the Subcommittee Director (Senior PSM)
Manage Subcommittee operations in the Director's absence (Senior PSM)
Maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders, including congressional leadership and industry representatives (Senior PSM)
Analyze and engage with the Department's annual budget (Senior PSM)
qualifications: Possess three to five years (PSM) or four to six years (Senior PSM) of congressional or relevant experience
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Demonstrated experience with congressional investigations
Deep understanding of the Committee process, House Rules, drafting legislative text, and oversight duties
Strong understanding of national security policy and the jurisdiction of the Department of Homeland Security
Active TOP SECRET security clearance (SCI strongly preferred)
Prior Hill experience is required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email their resume and a brief writing sample to chsjobs@mail.house.gov with "CHS PSM" in the subject line.
salary_info: $90,000-$115,000 per year (PSM); $115,000-$125,000 per year (Senior PSM). Benefits include health, dental, vision, and life insurance; retirement plan with employer match; student loan repayment program; transit or parking benefits.

office: Senior Republican Member, House Financial Services Committee (Name Unresolved)
description: A senior Republican Member serving on the House Financial Services Committee seeks a Communications Director to lead a proactive, fast-paced, media-centric operation in the Washington, D.C. office. This is not an entry-level role. The Communications Director will work closely with the Member, Chief of Staff, Legislative Team, and District leadership to drive a disciplined message across national, D.C., and district media.
responsibilities: Develop and execute a comprehensive communications strategy aligned to the Member's financial services, small business, and district portfolio (natural gas, coal, manufacturing, banking, capital markets, housing, fintech/crypto, insurance, monetary policy, access to capital) and broader conference priorities.
Serve as primary press contact and on-record spokesperson; cultivate relationships with national, regional, and trade outlets (e.g., TV, print, radio, digital, podcasts).
Aggressively pitch and book media; prepare and prep the Member for interviews and other media facing events.
Draft and edit press releases, speeches, op-eds, talking points, e-newsletters, and messaging.
Own official digital channels: content planning, copy, graphics/video guidance, posting, engagement, and analytics.
Manage an active social media with a presence on X, Facebook, Instagram, and Truth.
Coordinate closely with the legislative/oversight teams to translate hearings, markups, investigations, and floor activity into clear, timely communications.
Oversee message discipline and rapid response; develop crisis-communications plans and approvals workflows.
Maintain an editorial calendar; manage vendor relationships (design, video, monitoring tools) and track performance metrics.
qualifications: Pre-existing relationships with National bookers and producers is required.
Prior Hill communications experience required; experience as a Press Secretary or Communications Director strongly preferred.
Demonstrated on-record experience and sound news judgment under tight deadlines.
Exceptional writing and editing skills; ability to translate complex financial policy into clear, accessible messaging.
Fluency with digital platforms, media monitoring, and analytics tools.
Familiarity with financial services policy is a strong plus but not required.
how_to_apply: Please email a resume and a relevant writing sample to Matthew.Perricone@mail.house.gov, Courtney.Trigg@mail.house.gov, and Jackson.Notes@mail.house.gov.

description:
The Office of Congressman Pat Ryan (NY-18) seeks an experienced and creative Communications Director to lead a dynamic communications operation in a fast-paced, proactive, and collaborative office. The Communications Director will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive communications strategy that advances the Representative's legislative priorities and strengthens connections with local and national media, with a significant emphasis on digital as well.

The position will require working non-traditional hours. Ties to New York's 18th District are a plus.
responsibilities: Drafting and editing press releases, op-eds, newsletters, talking points, speeches, and other written materials
Managing the Representative's digital presence, including social media platforms, website, and multimedia content
Producing and overseeing video, graphics, and other creative content to expand the office's reach
Pitching the Representative for media opportunities, op-eds, interviews, and speaking engagements
Coordinating interviews, press conferences, and public events in Washington, D.C. and in New York's 18th Congressional District
Overseeing and managing a robust franking operation, including newsletters, e-blasts, and other constituent communications in compliance with House rules
Monitoring press coverage, providing media analysis, and advising the Representative and staff on messaging
Collaborating with legislative and district staff to ensure consistent, effective communication of policy initiatives and constituent services
Supervising press staff, interns, and district communications support
qualifications: Experienced and creative communications professional
Ability to work non-traditional hours
Ties to New York's 18th District are a plus
how_to_apply: To apply, please send your resume, cover letter, and 3 references in a single PDF to ny18resumes@mail.house.gov with the job title and your full name in the subject line (e.g., "Communications Director - First Last"). Applicants will be evaluated on a rolling basis.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation. Candidates of diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

office: House Committee on Education and Workforce, Minority Staff
description: The House Committee on Education and Workforce, Minority Staff, is seeking an experienced Press Secretary.
responsibilities: Assists with developing materials to communicate with the media, including press releases, talking points, emails, advisories, pitches, media kits, and reports
Works with the communications team to identify new ways to engage press and digital audiences
Pitches stories and fields a high volume of media requests related to the Committee's activities
Manages media-relations staffing (e.g., hearings, press conferences, roundtables)
Stays informed of current issues affecting the Committee and anticipates media inquiries
Prepares the Ranking Member for media interviews (including TV, radio, and print) and other public appearances
Works with key staff to develop and implement a comprehensive communications and media strategy that promotes the Committee's policies and priorities
Utilizes audio, graphics, text, and other digital media technologies to design, develop, and implement multimedia content
Helps manage all social media channels and web content to ensure all platforms are adequately maintained and up to date
qualifications: Bachelor's degree or equivalent combination of education and experience preferred
Experience on Capitol Hill and/or in fast-paced media environments is preferred
Experience with Adobe Creative Cloud tools (e.g., Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) is preferred
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should submit a cover letter, resume, two writing samples, and one digital sample (e.g., a social media graphic) in a single PDF document by Friday, March 27, to E&L.Jobs@mail.house.gov. The subject line should read: "[YOUR NAME] Press Secretary Application."
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including gender identity or sexual orientation), national origin, age, disability, gender, uniformed service, genetic information, or any other factor.

description:
The Office of Congresswoman Kat Cammack (FL-03) is seeking an experienced, strategic, and highly motivated Communications Director to lead and manage the Congresswoman's communications operation in Washington, D.C. The Communications Director will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive communications strategy that effectively advances the Congresswoman's legislative priorities, amplifies her voice across platforms, and engages constituents in Florida's Third Congressional District.

The ideal candidate is a strong writer, proactive strategist, and capable manager who thrives in a fast-paced environment and has a deep understanding of the modern media landscape.
responsibilities: Develop and implement the Congresswoman's overall communications and media strategy.
Draft and oversee the production of press releases, statements, op-eds, speeches, newsletters, and other written communications.
Manage national, regional, and Florida-based media relationships and coordinate press outreach.
Prepare the Congresswoman for media interviews, press conferences, and public appearances.
Oversee and coordinate all digital communications, including social media strategy and content development.
Manage and supervise the communications team, including the Press Secretary/Digital Director (if applicable).
Coordinate closely with legislative staff to translate policy initiatives into clear and effective messaging.
Monitor news coverage, identify communications opportunities, and respond rapidly to breaking developments.
Work collaboratively with the district office to promote constituent services, district events, and local media engagement.
Develop messaging related to key legislative initiatives, committee work, and congressional priorities.
Ensure all communications comply with House Ethics and Official Resources guidelines.
qualifications: 1-3+ years of communications experience, preferably on Capitol Hill, in a political campaign, or in government communications.
Exceptional writing and editing skills with the ability to produce clear, compelling messaging quickly.
Strong relationships with members of the press and familiarity with the national and Florida media environment preferred.
Strategic thinker with the ability to manage multiple priorities under tight deadlines.
Experience managing digital communications and social media platforms.
Ability to work collaboratively with legislative and political teams.
Strong attention to detail and sound political judgment.
Commitment to the Congresswoman's policy priorities and ability to communicate conservative principles effectively.
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should submit a resume to FL03JOBS@mail.house.gov
salary_info: Salary is commensurate with experience and House guidelines.

position_title: Military and Veterans Liaison & Community Representative
description:
The Office of Sara Jacobs (CA-51) is seeking a Military and Veterans Liaison & Community Representative to serve full-time in-person in the San Diego District Office.

This role manages a community outreach and federal casework portfolio on behalf of the office, including serving as the primary liaison to San Diego's military, veteran, and military family communities.
responsibilities: Meeting with constituents and local organizations on behalf of the Congresswoman and briefing the team on outcomes
Representing the Congresswoman at community events, meetings, and stakeholder engagements
Briefing and staffing the Congresswoman for district meetings and events
Building and maintaining relationships with military installations, veteran organizations, and community stakeholders
Coordinating with the District team and Washington, D.C. staff to support outreach and stakeholder engagement
Monitoring district issues, community concerns, and relevant local developments and updating office leadership and the Congresswoman as appropriate
Working with the District Director to plan and implement community outreach initiatives
Preparing meeting and event memos for the Congresswoman's daily briefing materials
Managing a constituent casework portfolio and assisting constituents in navigating federal agencies
Supporting district office initiatives and projects, including the Service Academy nomination process
qualifications: Strong ties to San Diego
Experience working with service members and/or military families
Excellent communication and organizational skills
Genuine desire to serve constituents contacting Congresswoman Jacobs for assistance
Bilingual ability is a plus
Flexibility to work evenings and weekends to attend community engagements and events
how_to_apply: Please submit a cover letter, resume, and three professional references to SaraJacobs.Resumes@mail.house.gov with "Community Representative - [Last Name]" in the subject line. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. No phone calls or drop-bys, please.
salary_info: Salary is commensurate with experience. Federal benefits are provided, including health insurance, retirement plans, and student loan repayment assistance.
equal_opportunity: The Office of Congresswoman Jacobs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status or any other factor or basis prohibited by applicable federal law.

description: Rep. Mike Levin seeks a temporary Caseworker based in his district office to advocate with federal agencies on behalf of his constituents. The Caseworker works with Rep. Levin's Director of Constituent Services to intake cases from constituents who have business before a federal agency, strategize about how best to help the constituent reach their desired outcome, and then execute that strategy. This position is based in Carlsbad and will end on 2026-08-01, with the possibility of extension through the end of 2026.
responsibilities: Serve as a representative of Rep. Levin while assisting constituents who need help with a federal agency
Work with Director of Constituent Services and District Director to execute responsibilities
Quickly respond to incoming casework inquiries and conduct casework intake process
Act as a liaison with federal agencies regarding assigned caseload and work to secure positive outcomes for constituents
Maintain clear and effective communication with constituents through phone calls, written correspondence, and in-person meetings
Monitor and update the Director of Constituent Services and District Director on casework trends and prepare reports on constituent issues and needs
Monitor and refer cases, when appropriate, to other government entities
Maintain accurate and complete files of casework and constituent communications in Intranet Quorum
Coordinate outreach efforts, including community events, to engage with constituents and gather feedback on local issues
qualifications: Basic knowledge of federal agencies and California's 49th Congressional district
Service-oriented attitude
Strong organizational skills
Proficiency at prioritizing and managing multiple projects at one time
Capacity to work both independently and as part of a team
Strong written and oral communications skills
Preference may be given to residents of the 49th District
how_to_apply: Please send resume, cover letter, writing sample, and three professional references to ca49.applications@mail.house.gov.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation. We strongly value the diversity of our employees and encourage candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply, including women, people of color, and members of the LGBTQIA community.

description:
Congresswoman Joyce Beatty (OH-03) seeks an innovative Communications Director who can design, produce, and turn around products that respond to the moment. Ability to anticipate problems and offer solutions, and familiarity with Ohio and national news outlets are key. This position is based in D.C., with occasional travel to Columbus during District work periods, and reports to the Chief of Staff.

The ideal candidate thrives in fast-paced environments, is comfortable working under deadlines, is confident interacting with reporters, is highly collaborative and eager to work with staff, and has a passion for politics and video production.
responsibilities: Develops and implements media, communications, public relations, and social media strategies
Remains abreast of current legislative and non-legislative issues
Proactively drafts remarks, talking points, and other content in the voice of the Congresswoman each day, in addition to quickly responding to breaking national and district-local news
Manages the Member's official website
Drafts regular commentary and op-eds for news outlets on important subject matter and current events
Creates videos, graphics, and other digital content that is compelling, engaging, on-brand, and error-free
Develops and executes press conferences and public events
Oversees the creation and distribution of the Congresswoman's newsletters and press releases, and produces franked communications
Maintains a good working relationship with the Congresswoman, staff, media, and constituents
Supervises and mentors press staff, interns, and district communications support
Works well under pressure and manages stress well
Accepts performance-based criticism and direction
Works a flexible schedule, including long hours, evenings and weekends when needed
Performs other duties as assigned
qualifications: Bachelor's degree required
Minimum 5+ years of professional communications experience required
District ties and a thorough understanding of the Columbus media landscape are preferred but not required
Strong writing, editing, and proofreading skills
Strong communication skills
Understanding of local and national news outlets
Thorough knowledge of the legislative process, procedures and organization of the House
Knowledge of video and graphic editing software and tools
how_to_apply: Applicants should send a resume, cover letter, writing sample, example(s) of graphic or video product(s), and three professional references in one PDF to OH3.Jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line "Communications Director - Name." Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. No phone calls, faxes, or separate emails please.
salary_info: Compensation is commensurate with experience, in line with House salary ranges. The office provides a robust benefits package. The position is eligible for transit benefits, student loan repayment assistance, and parking.

description: The Office of Congressman Gilbert R. Cisneros Jr. (CA-31) seeks a District Scheduler/Caseworker in Covina, CA-31. The District Scheduler/Caseworker maintains the Congressman's official schedule, travel plans, and related records. Caseworkers monitor and update the Director of Constituent Services and District Director on district and local issues. This position acts as a liaison to federal, district, and local agencies for the Congressman and constituents and answers casework correspondence and verbal communications with constituents.
responsibilities: Prepares the daily schedule for the Congressman and distributes copies to the Washington, D.C. and district office
Prepares detailed itineraries for the Congressman, including important numbers, locations and contact names
Prepares monthly projected schedules for the Congressman
Briefs the Congressman on all scheduling activities and requests of the district offices
Coordinates photo opportunities with constituents
Makes reservations for the Congressman's air travel, ground transportation, and lodging
Reviews the Congressman's mail and invitations
Responds to all invitations, either by written correspondence or by personal telephone call
Maintains files of invitations that have been declined, accepted, or that are tentative/pending
Ensures that the Congressman is provided with briefing materials for each event by coordinating with event participants and the appropriate legislative and/or district staff
Monitors the Congressman's incoming telephone calls, takes messages, and returns calls as requested
Maintains the Congressman's files, including notes, correspondence, and all information relating to travel
Acts as a liaison for constituents and other individuals when they visit the Congressman

Acts as a liaison between the Congressman and other Members, committee staff, White House, and government offices and agencies to arrange for the Congressman's attendance at meetings or to coordinate travel plans
Maintains a good working relationship with the Congressman, staff and constituents
Tracks the needs of office supplies and reports the supply needs to the District Director
Assists with mail opening, sorting, and distributing as needed
May be given an office issue area assignment to be in charge of
Handles casework assignments given by the Director of Constituent Services
Assesses casework for problems requiring legislative action and makes recommendations to the District Director and Director of Constituent Services
Acts as a liaison to local, state and federal officials and other persons or groups to form effective relationships for the Congressman
Performs other duties as assigned
qualifications: Bachelor's degree is required
Previous experience working for a Member of Congress, elected official, or government agency preferred
Previous office experience is preferred
Strong telephone skills
Strong oral and written communication skills
Ability to exercise discretion and independent judgment with respect to prioritization of and recommendations on appointments
Proficiency in word processing
Strong organizational and filing skills
Thoroughness and careful attention to detail
Ability to perform essential job functions above
Ability to work cooperatively and courteously with others
Temperament to communicate with a variety of personalities in a tactful, pleasant, and professional manner
Thorough knowledge of office administration, policies, practices, and procedures
Thorough knowledge of office computer applications
Thorough knowledge of office equipment and machines
Thorough proficiency in word processing and keyboarding
Ability to meet attendance requirements as established by the office
Ability to work well under pressure and handle stress
Ability to work a flexible schedule including long hours, nights, and weekends
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should send a resume and cover letter with the subject line "District Scheduler/ Caseworker" to cisneros.ca31.jobs@gmail.com
salary_info: $45,000-$50,000 per year. Employees may be eligible for benefits including Sick and Annual Leave, Student Loan Repayment Program, Federal Employees Health Benefits Program, Thrift Savings Plan, Federal Employees' Group Life Insurance Program, Federal Long-Term Care (LTC) Insurance, Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAFEDS), and Dental & Vision Insurance Program (FEDVIP).
equal_opportunity: The Office of Congressman Gilbert R. Cisneros Jr. is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex (including marital or parental status), military status, disability, or age. Pursuant to current appropriations law, to be employed by a House office in a paid position in the continental United States an individual must: (a) be a U.S. citizen; (b) be lawfully admitted for permanent residence and seeking citizenship as outlined in 8 U.S.C. § 1324b(a)(3)(B); (c) be admitted as a refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157 or granted asylum under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, and have filed a declaration of intention to become a lawful permanent resident and then a citizen when eligible; or (d) owe allegiance to the U.S. (i.e., qualify as a non-citizen U.S. national under federal law). Employment is at-will.

description: Congresswoman Nanette Barragan (CA-44) is seeking a Press Secretary | Digital Director to join our communications team. The Press Secretary | Digital Director will work under the office's Communications Director to carry out the Congresswoman's communications and outreach strategy. Ideal candidates are motivated self-starters, detail-oriented, highly organized, possess problem solving skills, thrive in a very fast-paced environment - and be willing to work extended hours and often times under tight time constraints. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply by 31 March, 2026.
responsibilities: Work with the Communications Director to build and maintain the Congresswoman's brand.

Develop and manage digital content across platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, Bluesky, and YouTube). Support the office's social media strategy by drafting social media posts and producing video and graphic materials.
Update and manage the official website.
Monitor national and local media coverage, as well as social media trends.
Clip and caption TV appearances, floor speeches, long and short form videos, and more.
Draft and edit press releases, advisories, franked communications, and other communications materials as assigned
Help plan and execute press events, interviews, and digital outreach.
Maintain press contact lists and support external communications efforts.
qualifications: Strong writing, editing, and strategic messaging skills
Skilled in Canva, Adobe Suite, or similar platforms
Media relations success and ability to assist with communications in a fast-paced environment
Los Angeles Metro | Southern California ties are a plus
Fluency in Spanish is a plus
Must be a collaborative, high-energy team player committed to advancing the office's mission and responsive to emerging priorities.
how_to_apply: Send (1) Cover Letter, (2) Resume, (3) 2 writing samples of 500 words or less, (4) digital/photographic portfolio, and (5) and three references (including name, title, organization, contact details, and your relationship. Please send all materials to CA44Resumes@mail.house.gov with "CA44 Press Secretary" in the Subject Line.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

description: The office of Congresswoman Nanette Barragan (CA-44) seeks a Scheduler | Executive Assistant. This position is located in Washington, D.C. Salary is commensurate with experience. This position requires availability outside of regular business hours including some weekend availability as needed.
responsibilities: Maintain the Member's daily and long-term schedules for both DC and the District
Organize and evaluate all incoming meeting requests and invitations
Keep the Member on schedule throughout the day
Work with legislative and communications staff to ensure the Member is prepared for events/meetings
Coordinate travel arrangements
Process and manage expenses and reimbursements for the Member
Support planning and scheduling for the Congressional Hispanic Caucus related to the Chairwoman
Facilitate weekly scheduling meetings with the Member and Chief of Staff to evaluate meeting requests
Coordinate with the Member and Chief of Staff regarding political scheduling requests
Communicate with constituents by phone and email to address requests
Drive the Member as needed
Perform additional administrative responsibilities as necessary
qualifications: Highly organized, detail-oriented, energetic, and capable of thriving in a fast-paced and rapidly changing workplace
Availability outside of regular business hours including some weekend availability as needed
Previous experience in scheduling/operations and/or Hill experience is preferred
Southern California | Los Angeles Metro area ties, access to a car, and Spanish fluency are a plus
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should send a cover letter, resume, and three references with "SCHEDULER" in the subject line to CA44Resumes@mail.house.gov
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

description:
Congresswoman Laura Gillen (NY-04) is seeking a Press/Digital Assistant to support the office's earned, paid franking, and social media strategies. This position is based in the Washington, D.C. office.

The ideal candidate will possess strong writing skills, experience with video and graphic design, an interest in traditional and digital media strategy, and a good sense of humor. Attention to detail is a must. Ties to Long Island are a plus, but not required.
responsibilities: Craft engaging social media content in the Congresswoman's voice to promote the member's priorities and respond to breaking news.
Assist with drafting and distributing press releases, advisories, constituent newsletters, statements, and speeches.
Work with the Communications Director in identifying new ways to engage with members of the press and online audiences.
Take high-quality photos and videos of the Congresswoman for compelling content.
Monitor and compile daily news clips mentioning the Congresswoman.
Maintain the official website and press list.
qualifications: Outstanding written and digital communications skills.
Experience with the Adobe Suite, Canva, or other content creation tools.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and think critically in a deadline-driven environment.
Commitment to understanding the Member's unique voice and messaging.
Strong visual intelligence and photography skills.
Sharp attention to detail.
how_to_apply:
To apply, please email NY04Press@mail.house.gov with "Press/Digital Assistant [Your Name]" in the subject line and the following application materials attached as a single combined PDF:
- Resume
- Two writing samples
- Three social media content samples
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status. Candidates of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

description: The Office of Congressman Steven Horsford seeks an experienced and strategic Director of Communications to lead a high-volume, high-impact communications operation. The Director of Communications leads the development and execution of the office's communications strategy, working closely across the team to shape and roll out messaging around legislation, major announcements, and public engagements.
responsibilities: Translate long-term strategy into weekly guidance, work closely with legislative staff on media strategies for bill rollouts and major announcements, anticipate emerging narratives, and enforce message discipline across all platforms

Secure and prepare the Congressman for national and local television, radio, print, and digital media, maintain a proactive pitch calendar aligned with legislative and district priorities, and build strong, trusted relationships with reporters, producers, editors, and bookers
Oversee digital staff and external vendors, ensure alignment between press strategy and digital amplification, and supervise the creation of official digital ads, graphics, and multimedia assets

Draft statements, talking points, op-eds, speeches, etc. in the Congressman's voice, coordinate closely with Member offices, caucus communications teams, and allies, and provide strategic counsel on the media implications of legislative actions while making sound, split-second decisions under tight deadlines

Plan and staff press conferences, tele-town halls, roundtables, and major DC- and district-based events; prepare and brief the Congressman for interviews and public engagements; ensure readiness on legislative and non-legislative issues; and maintain working knowledge of House rules, franking regulations, and compliance requirements
Directly supervise the Director of Digital Media and the Communications Specialist, and establish clear workflows, authority, and accountability across the communications function
qualifications: Previous communications experience is required (not an entry-level position)
Previous Hill experience is a plus
Demonstrated success securing and managing national media coverage
Experience integrating press, digital, events, and legislative communications
Experience managing staff
Familiarity with House franking rules and compliance requirements
Exceptional writing, editing, and proofreading skills
Deep understanding of print, broadcast, and online media
Strong political instincts and strategic judgment
Experience with digital communications, including paid ads and analytics
Ability to work calmly and decisively under pressure
how_to_apply: Qualified candidates should submit a resume and cover letter demonstrating relevant experience and interest in the role. All materials should be sent to the office's Chief of Staff, Yvanna Cancela at yvanna.cancela@mail.house.gov
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, sexual orientation, age, disability, marital status, parental status, military status, or any other status protected by federal law.

description: Congressman Steven Horsford (NV-04) seeks an experienced full-time Legislative Assistant for his Washington, D.C. office. The Legislative Assistant will manage the Congressman's portfolio for the House Ways and Means Subcommittees on Work and Family Support and on Social Security, as well as issues related to immigration, homeland security, judiciary, veterans, humanities, and other assigned policy areas. Prior legislative and/or Capitol Hill experience is required.
responsibilities: Collaborates with the Legislative Director to develop and advance legislative initiatives within assigned issue areas
Tracks legislation, hearings, regulatory actions, and policy developments; prepare briefings for floor activity, committee work, district engagements, and external events
Represents the Congressman at meetings with constituents, advocacy organizations, stakeholders, and senior government officials
Coordinates with district office staff to support community events, town halls, and local stakeholder engagement
Partners with communications staff to contribute to speeches, press releases, op-eds, talking points, and digital content
Serves as liaison to committees, federal departments, and agencies to advance legislative priorities
Acts as a liaison with committee and Federal departments and agencies to communicate and coordinate information regarding specific legislative matters
Prepares comprehensive briefing materials for hearings, markups, meetings, and votes
Drafts policy memos, legislative language, vote recommendations, and talking points
Additional duties as assigned by the Legislative Director, Director of Operations, and Chief of Staff
qualifications: Prior legislative and/or Capitol Hill experience required
Strong understanding of the legislative process, congressional procedure, and federal regulatory frameworks
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, persuasive materials for diverse audiences
Demonstrated attention to detail and commitment to high-quality, accurate work
Proven ability to anticipate needs and deliver results in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment
Skilled relationship-builder with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams and with external partners
Flexible, adaptable, and responsive to evolving priorities
Demonstrated commitment to continuous professional growth
Ability to foster teamwork, cooperation, and a positive office culture
A well-developed equity lens and a commitment to continuous learning and growth
Demonstrated capacity for strong attention to detail, ensuring all work is thorough, accurate, and completed with care
Ties to Nevada
Demonstrated commitment to public service
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should submit a resume, cover letter, and a one-to-two-page writing sample via NV-04 Legislative Assistant Application
salary_info: $60,000-$75,000 per year. Includes health, vision, and dental insurance, paid vacation days and sick time, student loan repayment, and transit benefits.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, parental status, marital status, sexual orientation, or any other factor or basis prohibited by applicable federal law. Applicants with disabilities who require reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or hiring processes are encouraged to request an accommodation at any time during those processes.

description: The Office of Congressman Tom Suozzi has an immediate need to hire a dynamic leader to fill the Chief of Staff role for the Washington, D.C. office in a full-time capacity. This is a full-time position. Candidates of all educational backgrounds are encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Serves as the confidential and strategic advisor for the Member to support legislative and administrative operations
Serves as the Member's chief policy advisor to develop all policy objectives, strategies, and operating plans for the Member's office
Leads and oversees all legislative, policy, communications, and constituent services strategies to support the office's legislative operation
Acts as the Member's principal liaison and representative with key stakeholders, including executives from the public, private, non-profit, and academic communities
Manages differing perspectives and opinions to navigate conflicts and develop solutions effectively
Organizes workload to prioritize tasks in accordance with varying deadlines and ensure work is completed in an efficient and effective manner
Oversees the hiring, training, and orientation processes of new employees and evaluates employee performance to ensure goals, activities, and objectives are accomplished
Delegates tasks to staff and supervises the efforts of employees including the legislative, press, and constituent service staffs to maintain effective office operation
Maintains oversight responsibility for salary decisions to assist with personnel management
Sets office goals, policies, and procedures, and monitors the office budget
qualifications: Coalition Building: Develops networks and builds alliances; collaborates across groups or organizations to gain cooperation, build strategic relationships, and achieve common goals

Decision Making: Makes sound, well-informed, and objective decisions; perceives the impact and implications of decisions; commits to action to accomplish organizational goals; maintains appropriate levels of confidentiality

Advising: Provides guidance and persuades others; utilizes give and take to make sure input is considered on important decisions; considers the legislative and political situation to provide valuable guidance

Managing Others: Plans, distributes, and monitors work assignments of others; evaluates work performance and provides ongoing feedback to others on their performance and opportunities to learn through formal and informal methods
Political Acumen: Identifies the internal and external politics that impact work; perceives organizational and political reality and acts accordingly
Candidates of all educational backgrounds are encouraged to apply
how_to_apply: Submit a resume to NY03.jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line "[Last name] - Chief of Staff." No phone calls or drop-ins please.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal-opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

description:
The office of Rep. Greg Landsman (OH-01) seeks an experienced, organized, confident, collaborative, and energetic communications professional to serve as Digital Manager + Press Secretary in a D.C. based, frontline Democratic Member office.

This non-supervisory, full-time position is a key member of the Communications team and plays a central role in advancing the Congressman's message across traditional and digital platforms. The Digital Manager + Press Secretary works closely with the Communications Director, Chief of Staff, Legislative Director, and District Director to develop and execute a cohesive communications strategy that strengthens the Member's public profile, promotes legislative priorities, and engages constituents.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, exercises sound political judgment, and combines strong media instincts with creative digital execution.

This position is based in Washington, D.C., in a standard office setting. The role may require carrying equipment such as cameras, lighting equipment, laptops, and other small office equipment. The position must meet minimum physical requirements (e.g., lifting, bending, crouching, standing). Reasonable accommodations are available.
responsibilities: Lead development, drafting, scheduling, and monitoring of digital content across platforms (X, Instagram, Facebook, Substack, Bluesky, email, website, etc.)
Translate legislative activity and district engagement into compelling digital storytelling
Oversee creation and distribution of weekly newsletter
Track digital performance metrics and provide strategic recommendations to increase reach, engagement, and growth
Coordinate rapid response content aligned with press strategy and current events
Ensure digital messaging is consistent with overall communications priorities and brand
Field and respond to media inquiries in coordination with the Communications Director
Build and maintain relationships with national, regional, trade, and new media outlets
Assist in developing and executing proactive media strategies aligned with legislative and district priorities
Monitor media coverage and develop engaging social media content
Manage staffing and execution of DC-based events, including press conferences, podcasts, TV interviews, and roundtables
Support the Communications Director in implementing short- and long-term communications plans
Collaborate with legislative and district teams to identify opportunities for message amplification
Assist in preparing the Member for media interviews and public appearances
Contribute to crisis communications and rapid response efforts as needed
qualifications: Excellent written communication
Excellent verbal communication
Video editing expertise
Graphic Design expertise
Attention to detail
Digital media expertise
Media relations experience
Political acumen and sound judgment
House and legislative knowledge
Planning and coordination
Research and analysis
Adaptability and initiative
Interpersonal and coalition-building skills
Teamwork and stakeholder service orientation
Decision-making and advising capabilities
Bachelor's degree or equivalent combination of education and experience preferred
Experience on Capitol Hill and/or in fast-paced media environments preferred
how_to_apply:
To apply, please email jobs.landsman@mail.house.gov with "Your Name - Press Secretary + Digital Manager" in the subject line (EX: Jane Smith - Press Secretary + Digital Director) and the following application materials attached as one combined PDF:
- Cover letter summarizing your experience and why you want to work in a Frontline member office, and any salient work experience
- Resume
- Writing sample which can include a newsletter primarily written by the applicant, op-ed, strategy memo, etc
- Sample graphic, invitation, flyer, poster, etc
- Video editing sample – a google or youtube link preferred.
salary_info: $68,000-$72,000 per year. Comprehensive federal benefits included.
equal_opportunity: Candidates of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

description: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez (WA-03), a distinctive voice in the Democratic party who is also the Administrative Co-Chair of the Blue Dog Coalition, is seeking a Digital Manager/Press Assistant to help amplify the unique interests of her district. This position reports to the Communications Director and will work across the team to elevate the Member's communication operation. Position can be based in the District or DC office.
responsibilities: Producing graphics and videos.
Using non-traditional storytelling tools to connect with constituents.
Getting creative with platforms like Adobe or Canva and deploying various social media platforms.
Assisting with newsletters, media advisories, speeches, and other press materials.
Maintaining the website and overseeing the distribution of daily press clips.
qualifications: Outstanding written and oral communications skills.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and think critically in a deadline-driven environment.
Commitment to understanding the Member's unique voice and messaging.
Sharp attention to detail.
Strong visual intelligence and photography skills.
Familiarity with logging, automotive, commercial fishing, or southwest Washington a strong plus.
College degree not required; non-traditional candidates are strongly encouraged to apply.
how_to_apply: Qualified candidates should submit a cover letter, resume, one writing sample, and one digital sample to WA03jobs@mail.house.gov with the subject line: "Full Name - Digital Manager/Press Assistant." Please share where you learned of this opportunity in the body of the email. Resumes will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled. The office does not discriminate. Please no phone calls or drop ins.

description: United States Representative Jimmy Panetta (CA-19) seeks an experienced and creative Communications Director to lead a dynamic communications operation in a fast-paced, proactive, and collaborative office. The Communications Director will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive communications strategy that advances the Representative's legislative priorities, highlights constituent services, and strengthens connections with local and national media.
responsibilities: Serving as chief spokesperson and primary point of contact for national, regional, and local press.
Drafting and editing press releases, op-eds, newsletters, talking points, speeches, and other written materials.
Managing the Representative's digital presence, including social media platforms, website, and multimedia content.
Producing and overseeing video, graphics, and other creative content to expand the office's reach.
Pitching the Representative for media opportunities, op-eds, interviews, and speaking engagements.
Coordinating interviews, press conferences, and public events in Washington, D.C. and in California's 19th Congressional District.
Overseeing and managing a robust franking operation, including newsletters, e-blasts, and other constituent communications in compliance with House rules.
Monitoring press coverage, providing media analysis, and advising the Representative and staff on messaging.
Collaborating with legislative and district staff to ensure consistent, effective communication of policy initiatives and constituent services.
Supervising press staff, interns, and district communications support.
qualifications: Strong writing and editing skills with the ability to translate complex policy into clear, compelling messages.
Experience working with reporters and producers; on-the-record experience strongly preferred.
Experience with video production, graphic design, or digital storytelling.
Familiarity with California's Central Coast, Spanish-language fluency, or multimedia skills are a plus.
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects, and respond quickly to breaking news.
Commitment to public service, teamwork, and advancing the Representative's priorities on behalf of the people of California's 19th Congressional District.
how_to_apply: Please send a cover letter, resume, a digital portfolio, two writing samples, and three references to ca19.jobs@mail.house.gov with "Communications Director" in the subject line. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.
salary_info: Compensation is commensurate with experience, in line with House salary ranges. The office provides a robust benefits package.

description: Congresswoman Michelle Fischbach is seeking a motivated, detail-oriented individual to serve as a full-time District Outreach Representative in her Moorhead and Willmar Minnesota district offices. This position plays a key role in community engagement and representing the Congresswoman throughout Minnesota's 7th Congressional District.
responsibilities: Build and maintain relationships with local leaders, stakeholders, and community organizations
Represent the Congresswoman at meetings, events, and roundtables as assigned
Assist with planning and executing district events and outreach initiatives
Monitor local issues and provide timely updates to district and Washington, D.C. staff
Support constituent services and special projects as needed
Assist with general administrative and office operations
qualifications: Strong written and verbal communication skills
Professional demeanor and strong organizational abilities
Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
Knowledge of or interest in public policy, agriculture, rural issues, or local government preferred
Proficiency in Microsoft Office required
Residency in or strong connection to Minnesota's 7th District preferred
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should submit a resume and cover letter to jack.vaughn@mail.house.gov. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.
equal_opportunity: Congresswoman Fischbach's office is an equal opportunity employer and encourages all qualified individuals to apply.
